title: Valanjanganam Falls
tagline: Valanjanganam Falls — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: Valanjanganam Falls, locally known as Valanjanganam Vellachaattam is a scenic waterfall located in Peermade in Idukki district, Kerala state.
overview.description: Valanjanganam Falls, locally known as Valanjanganam Vellachaattam is a scenic waterfall located in Peermade in Idukki district, Kerala state. It lies on the National Highway 183 (Kottayam-Kumily Road), about 1 km from Murinjapuzha and 5 km from Kuttikkanam. Tourists usually stop here for a tea and enjoy the falls. Its an ideal spot to stop and take a quick stretch and relax during tiring journeys.

topPlaces.name: Kuttikkanam
topPlaces.description: Kuttikkanam is a hill station in Idukki district, Kerala state, India. It is 3,500 feet (1,100 m) above sea level and surrounded by lush green tea plantations.

topPlaces.name: Paloorkavu
topPlaces.description: Paloorkavu is a small village in Peruvanthanam panchayat of Peermade taluk in Idukki district, Kerala state, India. It consists of around 500 residences and one church (St.

topPlaces.name: Panchalimedu
topPlaces.description: Panchalimedu is a hill station and view point near Kuttikkanam in Peerumedu tehsil of Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. On the Makar Sankranti day, many Ayyappa devotees camps there to witness the sacred Makaravilakku (holy flame) that appears in the Ponnambalamedu near Sabarimala temple.

topPlaces.name: Peerumedu taluk
topPlaces.description: Peerumedu taluk is one of the 5 taluks in Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. It consists of 10 revenue villages.

topPlaces.name: Meloram
topPlaces.description: Meloram is a village in Idukki district in India's southwestern state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Valliyamkavu Devi Temple
topPlaces.description: Valliyamkavu Devi Temple is a historic Durga shrine located near Travancore Rubber and Tea Company, 15 km (9.3 mi) east to 35th mile near Mundakkayam. The temple and its surroundings fall under the Peerumedu tehsil of Idukki district.

topPlaces.name: Peermade
topPlaces.description: Peermade, also spelt Peerumedu is a village, Grama Panchayat and hill station in the state of Kerala, in southwestern India. It lies 915 metres (3,002 ft) above sea level in the Western Ghats (Sahyadri) about 85 kilometres (53 mi) east of Kottayam on the way to Kumily.

topPlaces.name: Elappara
topPlaces.description: Elappara is a town in Idukki district in the southwestern Indian state of Kerala. It is one of the panchayaths in Peerumedu taluk.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Valanjanganam Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Valanjanganam Falls is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Valanjanganam Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (91 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (88 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Valanjanganam Falls?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Valanjanganam Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Valanjanganam Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Valanjanganam Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Valanjanganam Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kuttikkanam, Paloorkavu, Panchalimedu, Peerumedu taluk, Meloram.
